rankings.title: How to Get Your First 10 Customers
summary_bullets: First customers come from warm network.
Show up in person for early sales.
Use Reddit and conferences for outreach.
Outbound tools only after 10 customers.
Frame outreach as advice or feedback.

rankings.title: Recursion Is The Next Scaling Law In AI
summary_bullets: Recursion improves AI reasoning at inference time.
HRM uses hierarchical recursion with three loops.
TRM simplifies HRM, uses one network, smaller.
Tiny recursive models beat large LLMs on ARC.
Backprop through time limits recursion training.

rankings.title: Harshil Mathur: AI Is Compressing Every Moat
summary_bullets: Regulatory hurdles create deep moats in fintech.
Capital efficiency beat investor pressure for growth.
Early UPI bet gave Razorpay massive advantage.
AI compresses build time; speed of decision is new moat.
Founder mode beats manager mode for core decisions.

rankings.title: Inside YC's AI Playbook
summary_bullets: YC built internal agent infrastructure for all teams.
Agents given read-only SQL access to unified database.
Tool registry grew from 20 to 350+ tools.
Agents improve skills by learning from meeting transcripts.
Open agent conversations broadcast internally for transparency.

rankings.title: How The Best Companies Defend Against Mediocrity And Rot
summary_bullets: Shareholder primacy is a recent (1980s) invention, not natural law.
Mission-controlled companies (e.g., PBC, industrial foundations) outperform.
Best practices like independent directors often destroy value.
Founders can adopt legal shields (poison pills, PBC) early.
Novo Nordisk’s foundation structure saved its GLP-1 program.

rankings.title: How Meesho Became India’s Biggest Shopping App
summary_bullets: Meesho pivoted from local shop discovery to WhatsApp reseller platform.
Shifted to direct consumer app in 2021 after data costs dropped.
Now has 250M annual buyers, 1M sellers, 2.5B orders/year.
Uses AI voice agent to reach rural users who can’t read or type.

rankings.title: How to Build an AI-Native Services Company
summary_bullets: AI-native services replace outsourced human work.
Pick markets with low trust, low judgment tasks.
Founders need domain fluency and operational rigor.
Product is the operation; humans scale nonlinearly.
Avoid early demand trap; price per unit or outcome.

rankings.title: How to Build a Self-Improving Company with AI
summary_bullets: Roman legion organization is outdated for AI era.
AI enables recursive self-improving loops in companies.
Record everything to make knowledge legible to AI.
Middle management obsolete; everyone becomes an IC.
Software is ephemeral; business context is valuable.

rankings.title: Demis Hassabis: Agents, AGI & The Next Big Scientific Breakthrough
summary_bullets: AGI likely by 2030, says Hassabis.
Missing pieces: continual learning, long-term reasoning, memory.
AlphaFold-style breakthroughs need massive search space and clear objective.
Small models can be 95% as good at 1/10 cost.
AI for science requires deep tech, not just API wrappers.

rankings.title: The GPT Moment for Robotics Is Here
summary_bullets: Robotics data scarcity is being addressed.
Cross-embodiment training improves robot control.
Cloud-hosted models enable real-time control.
Vertical robotics startups now cheaper to start.
PI open-sources its models to spur innovation.

rankings.title: Self-Play for LLMs, AI for Biology, Formal Verification, and More | YC Paper Club
summary_bullets: Protein language models scale like LLMs.
Selfplay for LLMs plateaus without guidance.
Streaming RAG reduces voice AI latency.
Lean enables verified AI for math and code.
Agentic coding resembles real-time strategy games.

rankings.title: Zepto: How Two 17-Year-Olds Built India's Largest Seller Of Fruits and Vegetables
summary_bullets: Two 17-year-olds started Zepto during pandemic.
Pivoted from WhatsApp delivery to dark stores.
10-minute delivery model from first principles.
Largest seller of fruits and vegetables in India.
Uses AI for supply chain and ads.

rankings.title: How To Build A Company With AI From The Ground Up
summary_bullets: AI should be company's operating system, not just tool.
Closed-loop systems capture and improve processes.
Queryable organization enables AI-driven insights.
Software factories: humans write specs, AI writes code.
Three new employee archetypes: IC, DRRI, AI founder.

rankings.title: Tokenmaxxing: How Top Builders Use AI To Do The Work Of 400 Engineers
summary_bullets: Gary Tan returned to coding after 13 years using AI agents.
He built Gary's List and GStack with Claude Code.
"Token maxing" means spending heavily on AI tokens for quality.
Advocates "thin harness, fat skills" for agentic engineering.
Shipped hundreds of thousands of lines of code in months.

rankings.title: Inference, Diffusion, World Models, and More | YC Paper Club
summary_bullets: Five research papers presented at YC Paper Club.
Speculative decoding parallelized via SSD algorithm.
Diffusion models used for model predictive control.
World model training avoids collapse with regularization.
Data-constrained pre-training benefits from ensembling.

rankings.title: How Stripe Built Their New Website
summary_bullets: Stripe redesigned homepage after business evolved beyond old story.
Used custom tool to iterate wave gradient design.
Delayed launch to perfect animation quality.
AI speeds prototyping but doesn't replace craft.
“Walk the store” culture ensures user perspective.

rankings.title: The Most AI-Pilled CEO We Know
summary_bullets: Pedro Franceschi, Brex CEO, deeply integrates AI.
Treats LLMs as cheap, not precious resources.
Built "Crab Trap" network proxy for agent security.
Advocates CEO as chief AI officer.
Uses OpenClaw for personal agent automation.

rankings.title: Paul Graham, Founder of Y Combinator, Live from Stockholm
summary_bullets: Silicon Valley is current startup center.
Moving gives better peers and serendipity.
Valley investors decide faster than Europeans.
Going to Valley then returning helps Stockholm.
Y Combinator is optimal way to experience Valley.

rankings.title: Pick One Idea and Go Deep
summary_bullets: Overthinking prevents startup commitment.
Pick one idea, burn other boats.
Go deep enough to run customer's business.
Validate by customer pull and verticalization.
Failure mode is indecision, not wrong choice.

rankings.title: BillionToOne Is Solving One of Biotech’s Hardest Problems
summary_bullets: Prenatal test for rare fetal DNA in maternal blood
Uses synthetic DNA to subtract amplification noise
Stepwise expansion: prenatal → late-stage cancer → early detection
Founded by two PhD students with $300k
Now public, processing 600,000+ tests/year

rankings.title: Why Two IIT Engineers Turned Down $550K Jobs To Build A Startup
summary_bullets: IIT engineers rejected $550K quant jobs.
YC partner told them to pivot from edtech.
Won DoorDash contract with 8-person team.
AI agents for customer support.
Building "AI forward deployed engineer".

rankings.title: Zynga Founder: Consumer Is Not Investible Right Now - Thats Why You Should Build It
rankings.speakers: Thats Why You Should Build It
summary_bullets: Consumer not investable now, but opportunity great.
Mark Pincus shares product playbook “Proven Better New”.
AI enables new internet treasures; cost still high.
Founder mode means trusting instincts, creating context.
Fish are running: rare moments of product-market fit.

rankings.title: Emergent: How Six Months of Tinkering Led To A $100M ARR Company
summary_bullets: Emergent lets non-coders build software via AI.
Founder previously built Dunzo, a quick-commerce giant.
Reached $100M ARR in 9 months.
Uses multi-agent orchestration and custom infrastructure.
Advice: think global from day one, follow intuition.

rankings.title: Groww: If Your Customers Don't Love It or Hate It, You've Already Lost
summary_bullets: Groww started as robo-advisor, failed.
Pivoted to transparent mutual fund platform.
Achieved 100% organic growth via word-of-mouth.
Zero revenue for first four years.
Co-founders aligned on value system document.

rankings.title: Replit's CEO On The Only Two Jobs Left In The Company Of The Future
summary_bullets: Replit abstracts coding entirely via AI agents.
Non-technical users build real software on Replit.
CEO predicts two job types: builders and salespeople.
AI capabilities double every six months.

rankings.title: Conductor CEO Charlie Holtz Walks Us Through His AI Coding Setup
summary_bullets: Orchestrates multiple AI coding agents on Mac
Uses gooseeneck mic for voice commands
Enforces workflow: no direct file editing
Code is “sawdust” – prompts matter more
Spent $22k on tokens in one month

rankings.title: India Can Create The Largest AI Companies
summary_bullets: AI enables global companies from India.
Young founders gain advantage from AI coding.
YC values clarity, taste, agency in founders.
Open-source models lower building costs.
Projects (build + get users) generate ideas.

rankings.title: How Legora Went From YC to $100M ARR in 18 Months
summary_bullets: Legal tech startup Legora grew rapidly.
Used Jude Law in ad campaign.
YC batch helped fundraising and product.
Reached $100M ARR in 18 months.
Now building proactive AI agents for lawyers.

rankings.title: How to Make Claude Code Your AI Engineering Team
summary_bullets: GStack wraps Claude Code into AI team.
Office hours skill reframes startup ideas.
Adversarial review catches code issues automatically.
Browser automation uses Playwright for testing.
Thin scaffolding approach for agent workflows.

rankings.title: The Age Of The 40-Year-Old Solo Founder Is Here
summary_bullets: Ploy rebuilds old websites into modern, AI-driven marketing platforms.
Guest Bryant Chou (Webflow co-founder) demos Ploy’s design and SEO features.
AI lets experienced founders build solo, leveraging decades of taste.
Ploy integrates analytics, CRM, and agent-friendly outputs.
Demo shows 2008 sites transformed into 2026 versions in minutes.
